rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are valid AADL features or declared helpers in the same annex.
Use only visible AADL features or declared equation helpers in AGREE contracts; avoid introducing undefined or package-qualified identifiers.
When AGREE reports type mismatches, align helper declarations with the correct scalar types (int, real, bool) and ensure expressions match expected types.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, always validate that referenced identifiers exist in the current scope and are properly typed.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use assign for output bindings instead.
Ensure AGREE scalar type declarations match AADL expectations (int, real, bool) to prevent type resolution errors.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and placed in appropriate component type or implementation scopes.
Assignments in implementation scopes should reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Equation and constant declarations in AGREE must use valid scalar types and match the expected expression types.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types that are not present in the AADL model or its dependencies.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation-level scopes; use assign for output bindings.
Do not declare helpers with invalid or mismatched types that cause AGREE to report '<error>' types.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE annex placement in component types vs implementations
Assumption/guarantee syntax in component type contracts
